statement: The system shall allow integration of new diagnostic capabilities into the existing technology.
The system shall use a VR robot simulator with pre‑planned movement files to run simulations before operating the manipulator.
The system shall provide emergency stop controls positioned near the head, feet, and arms so that an emergency stop is always within easy reach.
The system shall stop the system within a tenth of a second when the emergency stop is activated.
The system shall execute predefined arm moves from files when a button on the computer is pressed.
The system shall detect out-of-sequence commands and either reject the input or automatically execute the next move in the sequence.
The system shall include Bolt Runner constraints to prevent cross‑threading when tightening bolts.
The system shall flag and avoid any perceptible delay, even as short as one millisecond.
The system shall break pre‑programmed moves into stages with intermediate waypoints and provide progress feedback.
The system shall prioritize frame rate over resolution to ensure a smooth refreshed view.
The system shall provide a ghost mode that displays a preview of the machine's planned path.
The system shall allow pre‑programmed no‑go zones and provide visual or audible warnings when approaching them.
The system shall provide a buzzer alert to the operator when the gripper is over‑gripping.
The system shall provide a 3‑D spatial image using a camera with an integrated light to aid depth perception.
The system shall enforce force limits and restrict arm positions based on the weight being handled.
